sourceMovie.title: K.O.
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: K.O. works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Southpaw
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If K.O. clicked for you, Southpaw (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Man with the Golden Gun
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If K.O. clicked for you, The Man with the Golden Gun (1974) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after K.O.?
faq.answer: Try Southpaw, The Man with the Golden Gun, The Green Mile—each shares DNA with K.O. while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is K.O.?
faq.answer: K.O. (2025) is best described as Action, Drama, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is K.O. worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
